keyPointsSummary: 'Able to' expresses ability, similar to 'can' but usable in all tenses
Formula: am/is/are + able to (present); was/were + able to (past); will be able to (future)
Useful because 'can' cannot combine with modals — you say 'will be able to', not 'will can'

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं यह काम कर पाता हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I am able to do this work.
She is able to run fast.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं कल यह पूरा कर पाया।
quickQuiz.answer: I was able to complete this yesterday.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम कल तक यह खत्म कर पाएंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: We will be able to finish this by tomorrow.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह इस समस्या को हल कर पाया।
quickQuiz.answer: He was able to solve this problem.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या तुम यह समझ पाते हो?
quickQuiz.answer: Are you able to understand this?
I was able to learn this.

quickQuiz.hindi: वे इस target को हासिल कर पाएंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: They will be able to achieve this target.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम एक साथ काम कर पाते हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: We are able to work together.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह पहले तैर नहीं पाता था।
quickQuiz.answer: He was not able to swim before.

mustRememberRules: 'Able to' expresses ability, similar to 'can' but usable in all tenses
Formula: am/is/are + able to (present); was/were + able to (past); will be able to (future)
Useful because 'can' cannot combine with modals — you say 'will be able to', not 'will can'
